content:
type: DEFER
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
spec: In dspy/clients/__init__.py, please add TTL support for my feature:
1. Add 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' parameter to configure_cache(), inserted alongside the other cache config params (e.g. after memory_max_entries, before enable_litellm_cache). Docstring: 'ttl: Optional time-to-live in seconds for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.'
2. Pass ttl through to the Cache(...) constructor call inside configure_cache() (Cache(..., memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) — adjust to match whatever final Cache.__init__ signature/kwargs ordering ends up in cache.py, just pass ttl by keyword).
3. Add a new module-level helper function:
   def set_ttl(seconds: Optional[int]):
       '''Set the default TTL (in seconds) for cache entries. Pass None to disable expiry.'''
       configure_cache(ttl=seconds)
   (or equivalent: it just needs to call configure_cache with the new ttl value while preserving other current cache settings if practical — simplest correct approach is to call configure_cache(ttl=seconds) using defaults, but ideally re-use dspy.cache's existing other settings if easy; if not easy, calling configure_cache(ttl=seconds) alone is acceptable).
4. Update the DSPY_CACHE = Cache(...) default instantiation to pass ttl=None explicitly (keeps behavior identical, makes the new param visible).
5. Add 'set_ttl' to the __all__ list.
I will implement the corresponding Cache class support (ttl param in __init__, TTLCache vs LRUCache switch, disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl) in put(), self.disk_cache.expire() call in get() miss path) in cache.py myself since I'm owner there — you don't need to touch cache.py for this.
note: set_ttl, configure_cache ttl param, DSPY_CACHE ttl=None, __all__ addition
